title: Pet Food - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Pet Food 39 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Foster Families Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Women BRIGHTSIDE ANIMAL CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter and adoption services for displaced animals, microchipping services, and a pet food bank that people can access every 3 months. Also sells Deschutes County dog licenses at cost. This is a no-kill shelter. All animals have been spayed and neutered. 1355 NE Hemlock Avenue Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday/Saturday 11am-5:30pm, Thursday 11am-5pm Email (541) 923-0882 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT ESTHER'S PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry, pet food, and personal care items that are available once a month. People without an HIV diagnosis may be eligible to receive a one-time food box of pantry staple items. Accepts donations of shelf-stable food, pet food, toilet paper, and personal care items. 10202 SE 32nd Avenue Suite 601 Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Individuals diagnosed with HIV who have a monthly income below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level Hours: Monday 1pm-4pm, Tuesday/Thursday 2pm-5pm, Wednesday 1pm-3pm Email (503) 349-4699 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS SERVICE CENTER FOOD PANTRY AND MEALS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food, to-go meals, home-delivered food boxes, and pet food when available. 8800 SE 80th Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Groceries (Food Pantry): Tuesday/Thursday noon-4pm To-go Meals: Tuesday-Thursday 1pm-2:30pm, Sunday 3pm-3:30pm Email (503) 771-7914 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP EMERGENCY FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry. Gluten-free items, personal hygiene items, and pet food may be available. Volunteer opportunities are available. 1103 Grand Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Follows USDA income guidelines Hours: Monday-Friday 3pm-4pm Email (503) 325-1702 Get Directions More Details COLUMBIA GORGE HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity to adopt animals, all of which have been spayed and neutered. This is a no-kill shelter. The society also has a food bank that provides pet food for low-income pet owners. 200 River Road The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Age 21 or older to adopt Hours: Tuesday-Friday 11am-5pm, Saturday 10am-4pm, by appointment only Email (541) 296-5189 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA PACIFIC FOOD BANK VERNONIA CARES FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Regional food bank for Columbia County; operates as a food distribution center for hunger relief agencies and programs. 547 Weed Avenue Vernonia, OR 97064 Eligibility: Must meet USDA Income Guidelines Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm Email (503) 429-1414 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COUNCIL ON AGING OF CENTRAL OREGON PET PALS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides pet food, pet supplies, and limited basic veterinary care assistance. 1036 NE 5th Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Meals on Wheels participants Hours: Pet food deliveries: Varies Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 678-9770 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DALLAS FOOD BANK FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry with a 3 to 5 day supply of food. Individuals may visit the pantry once per month and are encouraged to bring their own bags or boxes for transporting food items. Also has pet food. May be able to provide toilet paper, dish soap, laundry soap, and bath soap on a limited basis. Accepts donations of food and pet food. 322 Main Street Suite 180 Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines Hours: Pantry: Monday-Tuesday/Thursday 1pm-3pm, Wednesday/Friday 9am-11am Food Donations: Monday-Friday 9am-11am Email (503) 623-3578 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ESTACADA AREA FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Shopping-style food pantry provides a 5-day supply of food to households. Also offers pet food, hygiene items, household supplies, and clothing as available. 272 S Broadway Street Estacada, OR 97023 Eligibility: USDA-TEFAP income guidelines. SNAP, TANF, SSI, LIHEAP recipients automatically eligible. Hours: Pantry: 2nd and 4th Tuesdays of the month 10am-2pm, Wednesday 2pm-6pm, Thursday 10am-2pm Donations: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm, Wednesday 2pm-6pm Email (503) 630-2888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIDO PET FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides pet food for cats and dogs. Donations of pet food and supplies are accepted on-site and at various community locations. 14186 Fir Street Suite 203 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Low-income pet owners or individuals in urgent or emergency situations. Hours: 2nd and 4th Wednesday of the month 9am-noon, by appointment only, 3rd Saturday of the month 9am-1:30pm If food is urgently needed outside of service hours, please call to coordinate sooner pick-up if available. Email (503) 655-3663 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIDO PET FOOD BANK ANIMEALS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides dog food, cat food, and cat litter. Partners with Meals-On-Wheels to deliver to pet owners homes. 14186 Fir Street Suite 203 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Meals on Wheels recipients Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 655-3663 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIDO PET FOOD BANK VETERANS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides pet food for dogs and cats. 14186 Fir Street Suite 203 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Veterans, active duty personnel, and families of service members Hours: Wednesday 9am-noon, by appointment only, 3rd Saturday of the month 9am-2pm If food is urgently needed outside of service hours, please call to coordinate sooner pick-up if available. Email (503) 655-3663 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH FOOD BANK FISH FOOD BANK CASCADE LOCKS FOOD PANTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 3-5 day supply of emergency food. Pet food is sometimes available. Donations are accepted. 140 SW Wa Na Pa Street Cascade Locks, OR 97014 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: 4th Wednesday of the month 2pm-6pm and Saturday following 4th Wednesday 11am-noon. Check for holiday hours in November, December, and January. Email (541) 386-3474 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH FOOD BANK FISH FOOD BANK HOOD RIVER FOOD PANTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 3-5 day supply of emergency food. Pet food is sometimes available. Donations are accepted. 1130 Tucker Road Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday/Friday 3pm-5pm, Wednesday 9am-10am and 3pm-5:30pm Email (541) 386-3474 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH FOOD BANK FISH FOOD BANK PARKDALE FOOD PANTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 3-5 day supply of emergency food. Pet food is sometimes available. Donations are accepted. 4910 Baseline Drive Mount Hood Parkdale, OR 97041 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: 1st, 3rd, and 5th Mondays of the month 4pm-6pm; check for holiday hours in December and January Email (541) 352-3500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FORT KENNEDY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, pet food, clothing, showers, blood pressure checks, bus passes, legal information services, and benefit and housing resources. 7600 SE Johnson Creek Boulevard Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Veterans, all discharge types Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm. Various programs may have specific times. Email (503) 765-2661 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GREENHILL HUMANE SOCIETY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides animal adoptions; reunification services; emergency boarding in crisis situations such as domestic violence, medical emergencies, disaster response, and other situations determined to be such; spay/neuter services for feral cats; a pet food pantry; foster care; humane education; dog licenses; and volunteer opportunities. 88530 Green Hill Road Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 7 days per week 11am-6pm Email (541) 689-1503 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTLAND HUMANE SHELTER AND CARE HEARTLAND HUMANE SOCIETY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides open-door admission, low-cost adoptions, Trap-Neuter-Return (TNR) for feral cats, and working cat adoptions for shelter animals. Offers lost and found pet assistance, a pet food pantry, low-cost vaccine clinics, a safe housing program for pets of people in crisis, and low-cost end-of-life services for the community. 398 SW Twin Oaks Circle Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday noon-6pm, Saturday-Sunday noon-5pm Email (541) 757-9000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS COALITION COMMUNITY SOCIAL HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 2100 social services, nutritional hot meals, health behavior education, essential clothing, laundry and shower vouchers, temporary PO Box, and pet food, and resource navigation, tobacco, overdose, and suicide services. 4590 Florence Highway 101 N Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: People eligible under SNAP food assistance program Hours: Community Social Health Center: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 11am-1pm Hot meal: Monday/Wednesday/Friday noon-1pm Email (541) 997-5057 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIGH PLATEAU HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity to adopt animals, all of which have been spayed or neutered. This is a no-kill shelter. The society also has a food bank that provides pet food for low-income pet owners. 114 E First Street Alturas, CA 96101 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Wednesday-Friday 10am-2pm, Saturday 10am-1pm Email (530) 233-9277 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HUMANE SOCIETY OF CENTRAL OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides pet adoptions (all of which have been spayed and neutered), shelter, lost and found reporting program, HOPE Pet Food Bank, community cat program, and cremation services. Also offers Deschutes County dog licensing and renewal. Spay Neuter Assistance Program (SNAP) vouchers available that can be used at several different veterinary offices. 61170 SE 27th Street Bend, OR 97702 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 10am-5:30pm, additional times may be available by appointment Email (541) 382-3537 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LA PINE COMMUNITY KITCHEN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community meal, food pantry, clothing, blankets, personal hygiene items, and pet food. 16480 Finley Butte Road La Pine, OR 97739 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Meals: Monday-Friday 7am-1pm Pantry: Tuesday/Friday noon-2pm. Closed 3rd Friday of the month, open 3rd Saturday of the month 10am-noon. Clothing Closet: Tuesday/Friday 11am-2pm Email (541) 536-1312 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 223 SE M Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 841-1974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY ARCHES PROJECT ARCHES DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in day center with services including showers, laundry, hygiene kits, meals, mailboxes, and resource navigation. 615 Commercial Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Homeless individuals and families Hours: Day center and meals: Wednesday-Sunday 9am-4pm, breakfast 9am-10am, lunch noon-1pm Mail pick up: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm and 2pm-4pm Email (503) 399-9080 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEHALEM BAY COMMUNITY SERVICES NEHALEM BAY FOOD AND CLOTHING PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a pantry with food, cleaning, and hygiene items. Also operates a clothing bank for both adults and children, with new and used clothes, a limited amount of household goods, and medical supplies (such as walkers). Animal Haven is also housed in the pantry offering animal food and other items for pet care. Offers lunches to school students during the summer and school holidays. 36050 10th Street Nehalem, OR 97131 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Friday/Saturday 10am-2pm, Wednesday 1pm-5pm Email (503) 368-5612 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NO PET LEFT BEHIND PET ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance with necessary veterinary procedures. The amount will depend on available funding. Also provides pet supplies such as food, coats, and other items depending on availability. Eligibility: Individuals with low or no income Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 778-6328 Get Directions More Details NORTH COUNTY FOOD BANK NORTH TILLAMOOK COUNTY FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes with about a week’s worth of groceries, including fresh produce and basic kitchen staples such as cooking oil, sugar, and flour. Also offers cat and dog food, diapers for children and adults, and personal hygiene items. People can choose items from a list; selections vary based on availability. 230 Rowe Street Wheeler, OR 97147 Eligibility: Must meet USDA guidelines; no one is turned away Hours: Tuesday noon-3pm Email (503) 368-7724 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ON GOLDEN RESCUE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists horses, goats, donkeys, and other equestrians with routine veterinary care (veterinary, farrier: trimming and shoeing, vaccines, dental care), feed and supplements, assists with the construction of shelters and purchasing of bedding materials, limited emergency veterinary care. Based on funding availability. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 7 days per week 8am-5pm Email (503) 936-4396 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PET HELP FINDER PET RESOURCES LOCATOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Locator for veterinary services, pet food pantry, supplies, pet services such as boarding, co-housing shelters, pet care for seniors, transportation support, and behavioral help. Locator also lists grant programs, free and discounted services, and pop-up events such as vaccine clinics. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email Visit Website More Details PORTLAND OPEN BIBLE COMMUNITY PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry with canned goods, dairy, grains, produce, and meat. Household items such as diapers and wipes, pet supplies, laundry detergent, cleaning supplies, and paper products may be available when supply allows. Availability of items rotate and are provided through donations. Food donations are accepted. 3223 SE 92nd Avenue Portland, OR 97266 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Pantry: Tuesday 4pm-7pm, Thursday 10am-1pm except on federal holidays Email (503) 442-8228 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND OPEN BIBLE COMMUNITY PANTRY HOME DELIVERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides delivery of food items, including canned goods, dairy, grains, produce, and meat. Household items such as diapers and wipes, pet supplies, laundry detergent, cleaning supplies, and paper products may be available when supply allows. Preordering is available online in multiple languages, and navigators can assist those who need help with the ordering process. 3223 SE 92nd Avenue Portland, OR 97266 Eligibility: Age 60 and older or age 18 and older with disabilities Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 427-8741 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROCKN EZ RESCUE RANCH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity to adopt animals, all of which have been spayed and neutered. This is a no-kill organization. Also offers a food bank that provides pet food for low-income pet owners. PO Box 938 Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: By appointment only Email (541) 460-6420 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE VALLEY HUMANE SOCIETY PET RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers pet resources including trap/neuter/return (TNR) for community cats, pet food, humane education on responsible ownership and pet awareness, and lost and found pet assistance. Delivery of pet food once a month to eligible pet owners. 429 NW Scenic Drive Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Most services: Unrestricted Pet food: Low-income pet owners Delivery: Homebound seniors, veterans, and individuals with disabilities Hours: Monday-Saturday noon-4pm Email (541) 479-5154 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE VALLEY STREET DOGS PET CARE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides spay and neuter services, pet gear such as collars, harnesses, and bowls, pet food, and financial assistance for urgent veterinary care when available. Does not cover vet bills already accrued. Volunteer opportunities available and accepts donations of pet food, supplies, and gear as well as financial donations. Eligibility: Low-income households or homeless individuals Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 816-6035 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROSE HAVEN ROSE HAVEN DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center that assists with food, showers, laundry facilities, clothing, hygiene items, diapers, pet food, and temporary mailing addresses. Advocates are available to help connect individuals to resources and provide emotional support. Outdoor gear provided when available. 1740 NW Glisan Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Women, transgender, non-binary, and gender-expansive adults and their minor children Hours: -Monday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-noon for shelter, services from 8:30am-11:30am -Tuesday/Thursday 8am-3:30pm for shelter, services from 8:30am-11:30am and 1pm-3:30pm -Wednesday 1pm-3:30pm for guests with minor children only Email (503) 248-6364 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFEHAVEN HUMANE SOCIETY PET FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides wet and dry food for cats and dogs. The amount given depends on what is available. 32220 Old Highway 34 Tangent, OR 973899770 Eligibility: Pet owners experiencing financial hardship Hours: 2nd and 4th Wednesday of the month 9am-11am Email (541) 928-2789 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE HUMANE SOCIETY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an animal shelter that cares for homeless, abandoned, or neglected animals. Offers a pet food pantry when supplies are available and accepts pet food donations. The shelter maintains lost-and-found pet reports for Douglas County and ensures that all adoptable cats and dogs are spayed or neutered and microchipped before adoption. Pets are accepted for surrender only in emergencies and as space allows, and low-cost spay and neuter surgeries are offered on a limited basis. 450 Old Del Rio Road Roseburg, OR 97471 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Tuesday-Saturday noon-7pm Email (541) 672-3907 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES FOOD PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides fresh food boxes including meat, vegetables, dairy items, bread, baby food, and formula. Pet food is regularly available in the pantry. Limited monthly Senior Home Deliveries provided. People are welcome to receive food boxes two times per month. Also provides TriMet bus fares. The Community Garden is available to low-income families to raise their own vegetables. Water, greenhouse space, and gardening classes are provided. Gardeners give back by giving 10% of their harvest to the pantry. 17805 SE Stark Street Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Food pantry and community garden: Low-income households Formula: Age 2 and under Monthly Senior Home Deliveries: Homebound seniors age 62 and older Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-2pm and 6pm-8pm, Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 10am-2pm Email (503) 674-8785 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH BENTON RESOURCE CENTER FOOD PANTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry with canned goods, fresh produce, bread, and dairy products. Distributes pet food, diapers, personal care and hygiene products when available. Offers information and referrals to agencies for various assistance. Accepts donations of shelf-stable food, condiments, personal care and hygiene products, diapers, and pet food. 648 Orchard Street Monroe, OR 97456 Eligibility: USDA income guidelines of 300% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Tuesday 10am-noon 3rd Thursday of the month 4pm-6pm 1st, 2nd, 4th, and 5th Thursdays of the month 10am-noon Emergency: Call or email if outside of normal pantry hours Email (541) 745-8759 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK BAY COMMUNITY COLLEGE TBCC FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides canned and fresh foods, culturally familiar foods, pet food, and personal hygiene items, such as toilet paper, toothbrushes, and menstrual care products. All items are subject to availability. Donations of dry or canned goods, baby food or formula, animal food, clothing, diapers, and hygiene products are accepted. 4301 Third Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry: Monday/Wednesday 2pm-6pm, Tuesday 2pm-5pm Donations: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm (503) 842-8222 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMPQUA VALLEY HUMANE SOCIETY UMPQUA VALLEY HUMANE SOCIETY RESOURCE CENTER PET FOOD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides dog and cat food. PO Box 651 Winchester, OR 97495 Eligibility: -Low-income pet owners -Animals must be spayed or neutered. If not, arrangements can be made to have the animal's surgery performed. Hours: 24 hours a day / 7 days a week Email (541) 972-5039 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIGHTSIDE ANIMAL CENTER BRIGHTSIDE ANIMAL CENTER CASCADE AIDS PROJECT ESTHER'S PANTRY CLACKAMAS SERVICE CENTER FOOD PANTRY AND MEALS CLATSOP EMERGENCY FOOD BANK CLATSOP EMERGENCY FOOD BANK COLUMBIA GORGE HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION COLUMBIA PACIFIC FOOD BANK FOOD BANK COUNCIL ON AGING OF CENTRAL OREGON PET PALS DALLAS FOOD BANK FOOD PANTRY ESTACADA AREA FOOD BANK ESTACADA AREA FOOD BANK FIDO PET FOOD BANK FIDO PET FOOD BANK FISH FOOD BANK FOOD PANTRIES FISH FOOD BANK FOOD PANTRIES FISH FOOD BANK FOOD PANTRIES FORT KENNEDY FORT KENNEDY GREENHILL HUMANE SOCIETY GREENHILL HUMANE SOCIETY HEARTLAND HUMANE SHELTER AND CARE HEARTLAND HUMANE SOCIETY HELPING HANDS COALITION COMMUNITY SOCIAL HEALTH PROGRAM HUMANE SOCIETY OF CENTRAL OREGON HUMANE SOCIETY OF CENTRAL OREGON LA PINE COMMUNITY KITCHEN LA PINE COMMUNITY KITCHEN MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY ARCHES DAY CENTER NEHALEM BAY COMMUNITY SERVICES NEHALEM BAY FOOD AND CLOTHING PANTRY NORTH COUNTY FOOD BANK NORTH TILLAMOOK COUNTY FOOD BANK PORTLAND OPEN BIBLE COMMUNITY PANTRY PORTLAND OPEN BIBLE COMMUNITY PANTRY ROCKN EZ RESCUE RANCH ROCKN EZ RESCUE RANCH ROGUE VALLEY HUMANE SOCIETY PET RESOURCES ROSE HAVEN ROSE HAVEN DAY CENTER SAFEHAVEN HUMANE SOCIETY PET FOOD PANTRY SAVING GRACE HUMANE SOCIETY SAVING GRACE HUMANE SOCIETY SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES FOOD PROGRAMS SOUTH BENTON RESOURCE CENTER FOOD PANTRIES TILLAMOOK BAY COMMUNITY COLLEGE TBCC FOOD PANTRY UMPQUA VALLEY HUMANE SOCIETY PET FOOD
